learning_outcomes.description: Be conversant with of some the major aspects and themes of the Latin American intellectual critical tradition and critics/thinkers studied.
Be able to carry out a close critical and analytic reading
Think critically about texts and apply your knowledge to other issues and texts.
Think through and problematise the normative and received modes of apprehending Latin America and the world around us
Write a critical, argumentative essays which engage with the issues/ideas and texts studied.
Present an essay plans which critically engage with the issues/ideas and texts studied

assessments.assessment_title: Six in class handwritten reading comprehension Quizzes
assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 - WEEK 2 - 3/08/2026 11:00 am

Quiz 2 - WEEK 4 - 17/08/2026 11:00 am

Quiz 3 - WEEK 6 - 31/08/2026 11:00 am

Quiz 4 - WEEK 9 - 21/09/2026 11:00 am

Quiz 5 - WEEK 11 12/10/2026 11:00 am

Quiz 6 - WEEK 12 - 19/10/2026 11:00 am

All handwritten reading comprehension quizzes are scheduled IN-CLASS ON MONDAYS 11AM. This is a Secure Assessment
assessments.task_description:
All HANDWRITTEN READING COMPREHENSION QUIZZES ARE CONDUCTED IN-CLASS ON MONDAYS 11AM. This is a Secure Assessment. The schedule for the in class quizzes is as follows:

ONLINE QUIZ 1 - Week 2: 03/08/26: Mignolo, Walter. Preface: Uncoupling the name and the reference, The Idea of Latin America. Malden, MA ; Oxford : Blackwell Pub. 2005. (pp.: x-xx)

ONLINE QUIZ 2 - Week 4: 17/08/26: Sarmiento, Domingo F. Facundo, or Civilization and Barbarism (1845). Nineteenth-century nation building and the Latin American intellectual tradition : a reader / edited and translated by Janet Burke and Ted Humphrey. Indianapolis, IN : Hackett Pub. Co., c2007. (pp. 124-147)

ONLINE QUIZ 3 - Week 6: 31/09/26: Marti, Jose. "Our America", Nineteenth-century nation building and the Latin American intellectual tradition : a reader / edited and translated by Janet Burke and Ted Humphrey. Indianapolis, IN : Hackett Pub. Co., c2007. (pp. 260-267)

ONLINE QUIZ 4 - Week 9: 21/09/26: Boff, Leonardo. Part 1. 1. Ecology. Politics, Theology and Mysticism. Ecology & Liberation. A New Paradigm. Orbis Books: New York: 1995 (pp. 9 - 45)

ONLINE QUIZ 5 - Week 11: 12/10/26: Krenak, Ailton. Ideas to Postpone the End of the World. Canada: House of Anansi Press, 2020.

ONLINE QUIZ 6 - Week 12: 19/10/26: Rita Laura Segato, "A Manifesto in Four Themes", Critical Times: Interventions in Global Critical Theory, Vol. 1, No. 1 (2018), pp. 198-211.

Use of AI & MT in this assessment:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Handwritten, time limited quizzes to be completed in-person during class time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
14/09/2026 11:00 am

WEEK 8. This is an in-class presentation. This is a Secure Assessment
assessments.task_description:
Students will deliver a 5 to 7 minute presentation of their essay plan for Essay 1. The plan must include information regarding essay structure: introduction, body and conclusion. Contextualization of topic. Explanation and justification of thesis position. Presentation should refer to topics covered in class. Definition of key concepts grounding the essay. The presentation will be followed by a brief Q&A. Students will submit their plan (on PPP, for example) to their teacher prior to class on Monday 14 September at 11AM.

Use of AI & MT in this assessment:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be required to write a 2,000 - 2,300 (max.) word essay or critical commentary relating to a given text. Word count includes direct textual quotes. Questions and further instructions and guidelines will be distributed in class.

Use of AI & MT in this assessment:

Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
